feat: implement updateParticles with DAMPING=0.98 velocity integration (task 4.3)

Adds updateParticles(particles) which per frame:
- integrates acceleration into velocity: v = (v + a) * DAMPING
- updates position: pos += v

DAMPING=0.98 ensures velocities decay gracefully each frame, preventing
particles from accelerating indefinitely and keeping the simulation stable.
Unit tests verify single-frame decay, two-frame decay, acceleration
integration, and position update correctness.

commit.message:
Fix kings living in foreign kingdoms — relocate to own capital

installNewKing() never set kingdomId or townId, so succession could
crown someone who lived in another kingdom and leave them there.
Now installNewKing() sets kingdomId to the ruled kingdom and moves
the king to their capital (or any owned town).

Save-load fixup extended: checks kingdomId matches ruled kingdom,
verifies townId is in own territory, relocates to capital if not.

Per-tick enforcement in tickKingMood: if king drifts to foreign town,
immediately relocates them back to their capital.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: add SQL style guide with bad/good examples for all rules (#17)

## Summary

Adds `docs/sql-style-guide.md` — a reference for every SQL formatting
and lint rule currently enforced by jarify. Each rule has a **Bad**
(before) and **Good** (after) SQL example.

## Formatting rules covered

- Keywords uppercase
- Functions lowercase
- One column per line
- Leading commas (with first-column space alignment)
- AND / OR conditions on separate lines
- CTE layout (paren on own line, comma-prefix for subsequent CTEs)
- Table aliases always require `AS`
- Column alias `AS` alignment across a SELECT list
- Bare `JOIN` → `INNER JOIN`
- `IS NOT NULL` preserved (not rewritten to `NOT x IS NULL`)
- `NULLS LAST` suppressed when it matches DuckDB default
- `::` cast syntax normalised to `CAST(expr AS type)`
- Statement terminator on its own line

## Lint rules covered

- `no-select-star`
- `no-implicit-cross-join`
- `no-unused-cte`
- `duckdb-type-style`
- `duckdb-prefer-qualify`

Resolves #16

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: implement applyForces O(n²) with MAX_RANGE early-exit (task 4.1)

- Define MAX_RANGE=150, MIN_DIST=5, DAMPING=0.98, BOND_THRESHOLD=0.5 constants
- applyForces(particles, fc, w, h) zeroes ax/ay, loops all pairs i<j
- Squared-distance early-exit before sqrt for performance
- Uses toroidalDelta for minimum-image distances (toroidal space)
- Unit vector uses d (not dClamped) to preserve direction accuracy
- dClamped=max(d,MIN_DIST) only in the 1/r² term to prevent singularity
- d===0 exact-overlap pairs are skipped (no NaN)
- Newton's 3rd law: opposite accelerations scaled by respective masses
- Returns bond pairs where |F| > BOND_THRESHOLD (for later rendering)
- 5 console.assert unit tests: early-exit, in-range, toroidal, Newton's 3rd, d=0

commit.message:
feat: implement toroidalDelta with unit tests (tasks 3.2 + 3.4)

Adds toroidalDelta(a, b, dim) using the minimum-image convention:
  d = b - a;  d = d - dim * Math.round(d / dim)
This ensures force calculations always use the shortest path across
toroidal boundaries (e.g. x=10 and x=790 on an 800px canvas → 20px,
not 780px).

Also adds console.assert unit tests covering: the canonical 20px
wrap case, direct path, symmetry, midpoint, and same-position edge
cases.

   agent queue already exists in ADO via 'az devops invoke'. If it does, pass
   -var='ado_queue_exists=true' so Terraform uses a data source instead of
   trying to create it (which fails with 'Agent pool already exists').

* fix: label Cowork paths as 'Claude Desktop Cowork' in workspaceHelpers

Both getEditorTypeFromPath and detectEditorSource used a broad
'/.claude/projects/' substring check. Cowork session JSONL paths
contain that sub-path, so they were mislabeled as 'Claude Code'.

in both functions to return the correct display label.

The Pick<UsageAnalysisDeps, ...> on getModelUsageFromSession was missing
'claudeDesktopCowork', causing TS2339 errors in CI type checking.

Also adds workspaceHelpers.ts fix from previous commit to ensure
correct editor labels.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct Cowork session count, editor labels, and display

Three issues found via live testing:

1. audit.jsonl excluded: Each Cowork session dir has both an audit.jsonl
   (HMAC-signed audit trail) and a UUID-named .jsonl (actual Claude API
   session data). We were picking up both, doubling the session count from
   3 to 8. Fixed by skipping files named 'audit.jsonl' in walkForJsonlFiles.

2. agent/ directory excluded: The 'agent/' subdirectory holds background
   Cowork agent sessions (local_ditto_*) that the user never creates
   directly and that have no title. Fixed by skipping 'agent' directories
   during the walk.

3. enrichDetailsWithEditorInfo missing Cowork/ClaudeCode guards: Without
   an explicit guard, Cowork (and Claude Code) sessions fell through to the
   generic 'User' path-splitting logic, resolved editorRoot to 'C:' on
   Windows, and got labelled 'Unknown'. Added guards for both editors so
   editorName is set correctly before the generic fallback runs.

4. getEditorIcon: Added 'Claude Code' and 'Claude Desktop Cowork' entries
   so the orange circle icon shows instead of the generic 📝 fallback.

commit.message:
Feature client action throttle (#1301)

* Rename UserIntervals fields to English

Rename t_UserIntervals members from Spanish to English (e.g. Golpe->Hit, Magia->Magic, Caminar->Walk, Arco->Bow, TrabajarExtraer->ExtractWork, TrabajarConstruir->BuildWork, UsarU->UseItemKey, UsarClic->UseItemClick) and add related fields (DropItem, HitMagic, MagicHit, HitUseItem, Hide). Update all references to the renamed fields across the codebase: LoadUserIntervals initialization in FileIO.bas, interval packet writing in Protocol_Writes.bas, walk timing in Protocol.bas, and attack/spell interval checks in modNuevoTimer.bas. This change standardizes interval naming and keeps usages consistent.

* Remove per-user Intervals; use global interval config

Refactor interval handling: remove the Intervals field from t_User and delete the LoadUserIntervals loader. WriteIntervals now emits global interval constants instead of per-user values, and GM edit option for intervals was removed. Timer/interval checks were updated to use the renamed interval identifiers/constants. Also applied minor fixes: whitespace in WriteChangeInventorySlot and updated Server.VBP references to full paths and corrected module name casing.

* Update modNuevoTimer.bas

* Update modNuevoTimer.bas

* Update Declares.bas

* Add talk/left-click interval handling

Introduce IntervaloTalk and IntervaloLeftClick interval variables and propagate them through the load/send flow: declare new Long variables in Admin.bas, set them from PacketTimerThreshold using PacketNames.Talk and PacketNames.LeftClick in LoadPacketRatePolicy (FileIO.bas), and include them in the WriteIntervals packet (Protocol_Writes.bas). This enables loading server-configured rate limits for talk and left-click and sending them to clients.

* Change IntervaloOculto to Long; write IntervaloMeditar

Update Codigo/Admin.bas to change IntervaloOculto from Integer to Long for consistency with other interval fields. Update Codigo/Protocol_Writes.bas to include Call Writer.WriteInt32(IntervaloMeditar) in WriteIntervals so the IntervaloMeditar value is serialized/sent with the rest of the intervals.

* Update intervalos.ini

* Update intervalos.ini

* Update PacketRatePolicy.ini

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Lower many packet Limits and increase Iterations across PacketRatePolicy.ini to enforce stricter rate-limiting per action (e.g., CastSpell 1100→300, Walk 180→0 with Iterations 3→20, UseItem and Attack also reduced). Removed top-of-file header comments. Minor timing adjustment in intervalos.ini: IntervaloCaminar 215→210 to slightly change walking interval. feat: implement Fluxar chaosSign and chaosTick flip logic (task 2.4)

Each Fluxar particle now starts with chaosSign=1 and chaosTick=0.
tickFluxar(particle) increments the tick counter and flips chaosSign
(+1 <-> -1) every 120 frames, then resets chaosTick to 0.
This models Fluxar's chaotic behavior where force direction periodically
reverses (~2s at 60fps). Non-Fluxar particles are unaffected (no-op).
Unit tests verify: initial state, no-flip before 120, flip at 120,
tick reset, second-cycle flip, and no-op on non-Fluxar.

fix: add webui-router to e2e test suite and unify JS package build/test infra (#213)

The webui-router package was missing from the xtask e2e test suite, so its
tests never ran in CI. Two architectural contract tests were also silently
broken because they assumed esbuild's compact output format.

What changed:
- Replace esbuild with tsc for library builds in webui-framework and
  webui-router. Libraries should emit per-file ESM for tree-shaking, not
  bundles. esbuild is removed from both packages' devDependencies.
- Replace esbuild with tsc for unit test compilation in both packages.
  Tests only import relative siblings and node built-ins, so bundling was
  unnecessary.
- Remove esbuild from the webui-framework e2e server build. The playwright
  config now runs the server directly via node --experimental-strip-types,
  eliminating the build:e2e step entirely.
- Colocate webui-router unit tests in src/ (e.g. src/inventory.test.ts)
  to match the webui-framework pattern, instead of a separate test/ dir.
  Delete stale test/matcher.test.ts (referenced non-existent src/matcher.ts)
  and test/run-tests.ts (aggregator, replaced by glob pattern).
- Add webui-router as a PlaywrightSuite in xtask/src/e2e.rs so its tests
  run in CI. Relax the suite filter from playwright.config.ts to
  package.json so non-Playwright test suites are included.
- Fix two router tests that inspected handleNavigation source via
  .toString(): widen the abort-gate search window from 100 to 200 chars
  (tsc preserves indentation), and check for 'transition.finished' instead
  of '.finished' (tsc preserves comments that contain that substring).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix CLR->COM RCW ownership tracking on the slow path (#126731)

Fixes #126619.

This was a regression introduced in
https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/pull/114609

When `System.StubHelpers.GetCOMIPFromRCW()` misses the fast path on a
native-created thread, the slow path can create OLE TLS and still
resolve the COM interface pointer from the RCW cache. Those cache hits
are borrowed and should not be released by the stub cleanup path.

This change moves the `pfNeedsRelease` decision to the slow helper so it
can distinguish between:
- RCW cache hits after OLE TLS initialization
- freshly acquired interface pointers from
`ComObject::GetComIPFromRCWThrowing`

Testing:
- verified against the local repro from #126619

---------
